events.title: Act of Union (England and Scotland)
events.description: The parliaments of England and Scotland unite to form the Kingdom of Great Britain, centralizing political authority and economic policy.

events.title: Mughal fragmentation accelerates
events.description: After Aurangzeb's death, Mughal central authority weakens and regional powers like the Marathas, Sikhs, and various nawabs gain autonomy.

events.title: Qianlong Emperor's early reign and Qing expansion
events.description: Under Qianlong the Qing dynasty reaches territorial zenith, consolidating control over Xinjiang, Tibet, and Mongolia and sponsoring arts and governance reforms.

events.title: Seven Years' War (global conflict)
events.description: A global struggle among European powers with theaters in Europe, North America, India, and elsewhere; British colonial gains reshape imperial balance.

events.title: Early Industrial Revolution begins in Britain
events.description: Mechanization, factory production, and new energy sources transform production, urbanization, and social relations in Britain and later Europe and North America.

events.title: American Declaration of Independence
events.description: Thirteen American colonies declare independence from Britain, leading to the Revolutionary War and the creation of the United States.

events.title: French Revolution begins
events.description: Revolutionary upheaval overthrows the monarchy, leads to radical social and political change, and influences revolutionary movements across the world.

events.title: Napoleon rises to power
events.description: Napoleon Bonaparte seizes power in France, reforms administration and law, and leads military campaigns across Europe before his eventual defeat.

events.title: Battle of Plassey
events.description: Robert Clive's victory at Plassey establishes British East India Company dominance in Bengal, a turning point in colonial control of India.

events.title: Steam engine improvements accelerate industrialization
events.description: James Watt's improvements to the steam engine enable more efficient mechanized production and transportation, fueling industrial growth.

events.title: Post-Aurangzeb Mughal fragmentation accelerates
events.description: After Aurangzeb's death, regional powers (Marathas, Sikhs, Nizam, Nawabs) assert autonomy and contest Mughal authority across South Asia.

events.title: Sikh misls and Punjab polity formation
events.description: Sikh confederacies (misls) consolidate military and political power in Punjab, setting the stage for later Sikh unification under Ranjit Singh.

events.title: Qing consolidation of Xinjiang and Tibet
events.description: Qing military campaigns and administrative reforms integrate frontier regions, extending imperial control and shaping multiethnic governance.

events.title: Mysore emerges as a major South Indian power
events.description: Wodeyar and later Hyder Ali and Tipu Sultan transform Mysore into a centralized, militarized state engaging with European powers.

events.title: Qing economic and demographic expansion
events.description: Population growth, agricultural intensification, and internal markets expand under Qing rule, increasing regional trade and urbanization.

events.title: Battle of Plassey and British foothold in Bengal
events.description: Robert Clive's victory at Plassey establishes East India Company dominance in Bengal, a turning point in British colonial expansion in India.

events.title: Maratha Confederacy expands influence across India
events.description: Maratha chiefs extend control over large parts of the subcontinent, challenging both Mughal remnants and European companies.

events.title: Canton system and European trade restrictions
events.description: Qing authorities regulate foreign trade through the Canton system, concentrating European commerce in southern ports under strict controls.

events.title: European exploration and colonization in Australasia
events.description: British voyages (Cook) and later colonization initiatives extend European presence to Australia and the Pacific, affecting indigenous societies.

events.title: Ranjit Singh consolidates Sikh Empire in Punjab (precursor)
events.description: Ranjit Singh unites Sikh misls and establishes a secular, centralized Sikh state in Punjab that resists both Afghan and British pressures.
